category_title: Students with a history of school failure and resistance to traditional academic environments
minimal_context: The student is asked to write a formal summary of electrical safety procedures during a Professional initiation Social Guarantee Programme class.
dialogue:
TUTOR: Please write a formal summary of the electrical safety procedures we just reviewed.

STUDENT: Why do I gotta write this down? I can just do the wires and stuff. I do not think I am clever enough for this.

TUTOR: You need to practice your writing. Just write down what you know on the paper.

STUDENT: (Pushes the paper away) This ain't useful for nothing. I was forced to go to class anyway.

TUTOR: It is a required task for this program. Please pick up your pen and complete the assignment.

STUDENT: You just want to make me look stupid like always. The teachers were against me, and you're doing the exact same thing. I'm not writing anything.

options.A: Require the student to complete three revisions of the written summary, grading only the final draft.
options.B: Avoid forcing the student to write about electrical safety procedures, providing them with alternative topic choices.
options.C: Persist in building a relationship with the student and the group until a cohesive unit is formed.
options.D: Use a simple checklist to document the student's pragmatic language use during different class situations.
